assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet.
Developer has a backend web application ready for integration
Developer has basic knowledge of API usage and environment variable management

developer_goal: Send a first notification using Knock by integrating with the backend and creating a workflow.

first_success.outcome: Developer sends a notification through a triggered workflow that delivers a message to specified recipients.
first_success.observable_signal: API call to trigger the workflow returns success and the notification is sent to the intended recipients.

route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: Knock Elixir SDK
candidates.reason: While the Elixir SDK is official and documented, the primary onboarding flow documented centers on the dashboard and API usage, which is language-agnostic and more broadly applicable.

prerequisites.description: Create a Knock account and log into the Knock dashboard.
Have a backend web application environment ready to integrate with Knock SDK or API.
Obtain secret API keys from the Knock dashboard to authenticate API calls.
Set API keys as environment variables to securely use them in backend integration.

external_gates.description: User must create and log into a Knock account to access the dashboard.
Developer must obtain and correctly configure secret API keys for authentication.
Workflow must have at least one channel step configured to send notifications.
Trigger API call requires correct recipients and data payload to successfully send notifications.

platform_outcomes.description: Successfully trigger a workflow to send a notification to users via the Knock API.

sources.title: Documentation | Knock Docs

sources.title: Get started with Knock | Knock Docs
evidence_supported: Get started with Knock | Knock Docs
Add the Knock SDK to your backend

uncertainties.question: What is the typical or average time to first success (sending a notification) as claimed or measured by Knock?
uncertainties.impact: Time to first success is useful for benchmarking developer onboarding efficiency but is not explicitly documented.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: No explicit vendor claim or measurement of time to first success found in the official documentation.

uncertainties.question: Has the machine-drafted Knock route been independently shortest-path audited?
uncertainties.impact: Action counts and peer comparison must stay withheld until a human audit passes.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Complete a shortest-path audit against current official docs and mark verified only when evidence supports it.
